title: Notolepeum ceterach
intro: Notolepia ceterach, previously known as Notolepia ceterach, is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues and improve wound healing. The bioactive constituents of Notolepia ceterach include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. Notolepia ceterach is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including bronchitis, asthma, and skin infections.
taxonomy: Notolepeum ceterach is classified as a genus of ferns in the family Polypodiaceae. Notolepeum ceterach is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The classification of Notolepeum ceterach places it within the division Pteridophyta and the class Polypodiopsida. This fern genus is relatively small, consisting of a limited number of species.
